name: Nat Hentoff
abstract: Nathan Irving Hentoff (June 10, 1925 – January 7, 2017) was an American historian, novelist, jazz and country music critic, and syndicated columnist for United Media. Hentoff was a columnist for The Village Voice from 1958 to 2009.

interviews.title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: Nat Hentoff wrote the liner notes, because he was a Boston D.J. at that time and a good friend of mine.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: And right around that time I met a couple of writers for Down Beat, Nat Hentoff I think was still living in Boston... eventually somebody asked me to do an article for Down Beat... about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: ‘Cause you called Nat Hentoff and you said, ‘I got all this Joe Williams stuff,” and Nat said, “Call Joel.” You and I spoke the next thing we know we have “Havin’ a Good Time” with Joe Williams.
